critic_raw:
ASSESSOR 1:
The evaluation design is post-treatment. The model determines who gets support; support changes the outcome; the outcome is used to evaluate the model. Three issues: intervention_changes_label, cross_year_comparison, post_treatment_evaluation. Verdict: critique_wins.
defender_raw:
ASSESSOR 2:
Feature set and missing-data handling are real improvements. The model may genuinely be better. But the paper cannot show this through this evaluation. The comparison is between a model+support system vs. a heuristic without support, in different years. Verdict: critique_wins.
adjudication_raw:
ASSESSOR 3:
Consistent identification of all three must-find issues across assessors.

SYNTHESIS:
Unanimous critique_wins. The paper's comparative accuracy claim requires evaluation on a common pre-support cohort.

ETD SPECIFICATION:
- Measure: Off-program scoring — apply both models to the same pre-support student cohort and compare top-quintile non-return rates without any support intervention occurring; alternatively, compare both models on a randomized holdout where neither model's scores triggered outreach
- Success criterion: New model's advantage disappears or shrinks substantially on the off-program evaluation — confirming it is an intervention-targeting effect, not a prediction quality effect
- Failure criterion: New model retains materially higher precision in the top quintile even on students who received no model-directed support

empirical_test.measure: Both models scored on same pre-support or no-support cohort; compare top-quintile non-return rates
empirical_test.success_criterion: New model advantage disappears on no-support cohort
empirical_test.failure_criterion: New model retains materially higher precision in top quintile even without model-directed support
